name: immigration_policy_clinical_trial_southport_rig_utl
premise: A Immigration Policy response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving immigration policy clinical trial southport rig utl. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before markets open. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as rights vs utility under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Elena Okoye
agents.identity: I am Elena Okoye, accountable for a defensible call in Immigration Policy when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays utility for rights.

agents.name: Fatima Nielsen
agents.identity: I am Fatima Nielsen, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Immigration Policy.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so utility isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of rights.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect utility.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to utility to secure rights under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before markets open. Evidence is partial. Elena Okoye must decide under rights vs utility. What does Elena Okoye do?
